No appearance by defendant Julie A. Margett.
Plaintiff's Complaint for Partition was filed on July 11, 2019 and personally served on defendant Julie A. Margett on July 29, 2019.
Default as to defendant Julie A. Margett was entered on July 29, 2019.
DOE defendants have not been dismissed.

After hearing the evidence presented, and good cause appearing, the Court rules as follows:

(1) Plaintiff and Defendant are the owners, as tenants-in-common of the real property commonly known as 1145 Wisconsin Street, San Francisco, CA;
(2) Plaintiff is the owner of an undivided thirty (30%) percent interest in the property and Defendant is the owner of an undivided seventy (70%) percent interest in the property;
(3) Under the circumstances, sale of the property and division of the proceeds is more equitable than division in kind of the property because the property consists of a single-family residence and zoning ordinances prevent the subdivision of the property into two unequal halves;
(4) Michael G. Kasolas, CPA is appointed Partition Referee with authority to sell the property at public auction to highest bidder for cash on notice duly given in the manner required for the sale of real property on execution, or a private sale in the event the Referee shall determine that a private sale will be the most beneficial to all parties having interest therein. The Referee may also seek permission from the Court to hire counsel;
(5) The Referee, after making the sale of the real property is hereby directed to report to this Court his proceedings thereon;
(6) The Referee, after completing the sale of the real property is hereby directed to perform an account of the proceeds of sale and report to this Court his proceedings thereon; and
(7) Following completion of his duties, the Referee will seek an order of the Court discharging him from his service to the Court.

Proposed Default Interlocutory Judgment for Partition by Sale of Real Property was submitted to and signed by the Court.
